value.body:
## Outcome

Make Cycle the control plane for agent-executed development work: users direct and approve work, agents claim dependency-ready tickets, run in isolated worktrees, update ticket state through MCP, produce verification evidence, push a branch, and leave a merge-ready handoff for the user.

## Ideal execution environment

- User creates, prioritizes, and approves tickets.
- Cycle selects or recommends the next dependency-ready ticket by priority.
- Agent receives one ticket, repository context, dependency context, allowed tools, approval mode, and worktree path.
- Agent updates ticket state, comments, records, logs, and follow-up tickets through typed MCP tools.
- Agent finishes by pushing a branch and attaching a concise handoff with diff, tests, artifacts, risks, and merge instructions.
- User performs only final review and merge from the branch.

## Acceptance criteria

- Agent assignment and execution do not rely on hidden chat-only state.
- MCP exposes enough typed operations for ticket lifecycle, dependency, run-state, artifact, and handoff updates.
- Tickets can express dependencies/blockers and the queue can sort by priority plus dependency readiness.
- The human final-merge gate is explicit and supported by UI and API state.

frontmatter.title: Roadmap: Agent execution control plane and MCP ergonomics

value.title: Roadmap: Agent execution control plane and MCP ergonomics
